nodes.label: Bucca: "Does skin color affect educational and labor market outcomes in the United States?"
nodes.author: Mauricio Bucca

nodes.label: Concept: colorism / gradational (phenotype-based) racial stratification
nodes.rationale: Central organizing concept of the paper: a system of social valuation privileging lighter skin over darker skin that operates alongside (and partly independent of) categorical race, motivating the paper's causal identification strategy (regression adjustment + sibling fixed effects) to separate skin-tone discrimination from inherited disadvantage.
